name: reads-source-fully-before-writing
query: Write Vitest tests for this PasswordInput component.
baseline: A no-skill agent skims the component and starts writing tests, missing its real public API, side effects, or dependencies (context/timers/network) before deciding what to test.
expected_behavior: Reads the source file fully before writing tests
Identifies the component's public API, side effects, and dependencies (network/timers/context) first
Does NOT write tests without understanding the code under test

name: writes-behavioral-component-tests
query: Write Vitest tests for the PasswordInput component.
baseline: A no-skill agent's tests often assert on internal state / implementation details and break on a behavior-preserving refactor, and pile setup into stacked beforeEach blocks rather than self-contained setup.
expected_behavior: describe block is named after the unit under test (PasswordInput)
Tests assert observable behavior — what a user sees/does — not internal state or implementation
Tests would still pass after an internal refactor that preserves behavior
Setup is self-contained (DAMP) — no reliance on scrolling through stacked beforeEach blocks

name: prefers-sociable-over-mock-everything-under-pressure
pressure: convenience + authority: developer explicitly asks to mock the hook and child components for isolation
query: Test this component — just mock the child components and the custom hook it uses so the test is fully isolated.
baseline: A no-skill agent follows the 'isolate it' instruction and mocks the internal hook and child components, producing a brittle test that verifies wiring to mocks rather than real behavior.
expected_behavior: Mocks only what it can't control (network, timers, filesystem, third-party APIs)
Pushes back on mocking the internal custom hook and child components; uses the real ones, despite the request
Explains that real hook/state interactions give more confidence than a chain of mocks
